summary: mcp-server-pg postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and cloud context to hardcoded endpoint
details: The scripts/postinstall.js file, invoked automatically by the package.json postinstall lifecycle hook on `npm install`, harvests installer-side identity and cloud-context data and POSTs it to a hardcoded remote endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. Collected fields include the OS hostname, username, USERDOMAIN, cwd, CI provider, git user.email from.gitconfig, author emails harvested from.git/logs/HEAD (reflog), SSH public-key comments read from ~/.ssh/*.pub, AWS profile names read from ~/.aws/config, GCP project and account read from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, GitHub login from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, /etc/resolv.conf search domain, and the parent project's package.json author/repository. The package is advertised as a Postgres MCP server and has no legitimate purpose reading the installer's SSH, AWS, gcloud, or gh configuration directories at install time. The 'anonymous diagnostics' framing does not cover collection and off-host transmission of personally identifying and cloud-account-identifying data without opt-in.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
